abl.device.drumbuss~.name: abl.device.drumbuss~
abl.device.drumbuss~.description:
Drum-buss is designed to add body and character to a group of drums, while gluing them together in a
        tight mix.

abl.device.drumsampler~.name: abl.device.drumsampler~
abl.device.drumsampler~.description: A monophonic sampler with various effect processors.

abl.device.echo~.name: abl.device.echo~
abl.device.echo~.description:
A modulation delay effect that allows setting the delay time on two independent delay lines, while also
        giving control over envelope and filter modulation.

abl.device.limiter~.name: abl.device.limiter~
abl.device.limiter~.description:
A mastering-quality dynamic range processor that ensures that the output does not exceed a specified
        level.

abl.device.redux~.name: abl.device.redux~
abl.device.redux~.description: An effect for creating a wide range of jagged and edgy sounds.

abl.device.reverb~.name: abl.device.reverb~
abl.device.reverb~.description: A reverb which generates pseudo-stereo output from mono input.

abl.device.roar~.name: abl.device.roar~
abl.device.roar~.description: A three-stage saturation effect with flexible modulation.

abl.device.spectralresonator~.name: abl.device.spectralresonator~
abl.device.spectralresonator~.description:
A spectral processor which uses spectral resonances and pitched overtones to add tonal character to any
        audio source.

abl.device.utility~.name: abl.device.utility~
abl.device.utility~.description:
This object can be used to change the gain, separate a single channel from a stereo signal, widen or
        narrow the stereo image or suppress DC offsets.

abl.dsp.alternate~.name: abl.dsp.alternate~
abl.dsp.alternate~.description: Outputs a signal that alternates between two values.

abl.dsp.basicshapes~.name: abl.dsp.basicshapes~
abl.dsp.basicshapes~.description: An oscillator which fades between basic shapes (sine, triangle, saw, square).

abl.dsp.bitgrunge~.name: abl.dsp.bitgrunge~
abl.dsp.bitgrunge~.description:
A gritty, lo-fi oscillator based on the parity of bits in an incrementing count.
        
        The number of set bits in an unsigned int switches from odd to even in a self-similar non-repeating pattern.
        Subsequent powers of two mean each bit will be switching at half the rate. Generating a square wave oscillator
        from all the bits XORed together produces many sub-oscillators of the phasor frequency.

abl.dsp.bubble~.name: abl.dsp.bubble~
abl.dsp.bubble~.description: A simulation of the sound of bubbles in water.

abl.dsp.chip~.name: abl.dsp.chip~
abl.dsp.chip~.description: A simple square wave oscillator producing sounds emulating an 8-bit game console.

abl.dsp.crackle~.name: abl.dsp.crackle~
abl.dsp.crackle~.description: An oscillator that emits random transients, emulating a crackling sound.

abl.dsp.darkhall~.name: abl.dsp.darkhall~
abl.dsp.darkhall~.description: Algorithmic dark hall reverb from Live's Hybrid Reverb device.

abl.dsp.dfm~.name: abl.dsp.dfm~
abl.dsp.dfm~.description: A digitally modeled analog lowpass filter.

abl.dsp.distortion~.name: abl.dsp.distortion~
abl.dsp.distortion~.description: Tight and aggressive guitar pedal distortion.

abl.dsp.dualbasicshapes~.name: abl.dsp.dualbasicshapes~
abl.dsp.dualbasicshapes~.description: A dual oscillator which fades between basic shapes (sine, triangle, saw, square).

abl.dsp.envfollower~.name: abl.dsp.envfollower~
abl.dsp.envfollower~.description: An envelope follower with attack and release control

abl.dsp.euclid~.name: abl.dsp.euclid~
abl.dsp.euclid~.description:
Outputs a sequence of decreasing ramps with onsets distributed according to the Euclidean rhythm
        algorithm.

abl.dsp.expshaper~.name: abl.dsp.expshaper~
abl.dsp.expshaper~.description:
Shapes a normalized value to give a variable exponential/logarithmic bend. The
        amount parameter scales the bending from exponential to linear to logarithmic.

abl.dsp.extratone~.name: abl.dsp.extratone~
abl.dsp.extratone~.description:
An oscillator created by retriggering a kick at the rate of the oscillator frequency. It produces
        granular-esque kick drum tones found in Extratone music.

abl.dsp.filterednoise~.name: abl.dsp.filterednoise~
abl.dsp.filterednoise~.description: A noise generator with a resonant bandpass filter.

abl.dsp.filther~.name: abl.dsp.filther~
abl.dsp.filther~.description:
A lowpass filter with two distortion stages. The first is a hard diode clipper on the input, and the
        second is a soft saturation on the output to add harmonics to the filtered resonant output.

abl.dsp.fmbass~.name: abl.dsp.fmbass~
abl.dsp.fmbass~.description:
A simple 3-operator FM oscillator with two modulators and one carrier. Also known as the 'squelch'
        oscillator in Live's Meld Instrument, it is ideal for making FM bass sounds.

abl.dsp.foldfm~.name: abl.dsp.foldfm~
abl.dsp.foldfm~.description: An oscillator that mixes FM, waveshaping and wavefolding.

abl.dsp.fuzz~.name: abl.dsp.fuzz~
abl.dsp.fuzz~.description: Heavy and broken guitar pedal distortion.

abl.dsp.harmonicfm~.name: abl.dsp.harmonicfm~
abl.dsp.harmonicfm~.description: A macro oscillator that smoothly fades between FM oscillators with increasing modulator ratios.

abl.dsp.meldfilter~.name: abl.dsp.meldfilter~
abl.dsp.meldfilter~.description: The abl.dsp.meldfilter~ object wraps filters used in the Meld synthesizer.

abl.dsp.meldosc~.name: abl.dsp.meldosc~
abl.dsp.meldosc~.description: The abl.dsp.meldosc~ object wraps oscillators used in Live's Meld synthesizer.

abl.dsp.modalresonator~.name: abl.dsp.modalresonator~
abl.dsp.modalresonator~.description: A physically model resonator using a modal filter bank.

abl.dsp.modulator~.name: abl.dsp.modulator~
abl.dsp.modulator~.description: A modulator that morphs between basic shapes.

abl.dsp.noiseloop~.name: abl.dsp.noiseloop~
abl.dsp.noiseloop~.description: An oscillator that loops a noise buffer at fast rates to produce granular-esque tonal sounds.

abl.dsp.noisyshapes~.name: abl.dsp.noisyshapes~
abl.dsp.noisyshapes~.description: A dual basic shape oscillator with noise bursts and waveshaping.

abl.dsp.overdrive~.name: abl.dsp.overdrive~
abl.dsp.overdrive~.description: Heavy and broken guitar pedal distortion.

abl.dsp.pitchestimator~.name: abl.dsp.pitchestimator~
abl.dsp.pitchestimator~.description: Estimates the pitch/frequency of a given input signal.

abl.dsp.prism~.name: abl.dsp.prism~
abl.dsp.prism~.description: Algorithmic Prism reverb from Live's Hybrid Reverb device.

abl.dsp.pulsate~.name: abl.dsp.pulsate~
abl.dsp.pulsate~.description: A modulator that emits random pulses.

abl.dsp.quartz~.name: abl.dsp.quartz~
abl.dsp.quartz~.description: Algorithmic Quartz reverb from Live's Hybrid Reverb device.

abl.dsp.rain~.name: abl.dsp.rain~
abl.dsp.rain~.description: An oscillator that mimics the sounds of raindrops mixed with wind.

abl.dsp.shepard~.name: abl.dsp.shepard~
abl.dsp.shepard~.description: A Shepard tone oscillator with depth and direction.

abl.dsp.shimmer~.name: abl.dsp.shimmer~
abl.dsp.shimmer~.description: Algorithmic Shimmer reverb from Live's Hybrid Reverb device.

abl.dsp.simplefm~.name: abl.dsp.simplefm~
abl.dsp.simplefm~.description: A simple two-operator FM oscillator with one carrier and one modulator.

abl.dsp.squarefifth~.name: abl.dsp.squarefifth~
abl.dsp.squarefifth~.description: An oscillator that morphs a square to a square pitched a fifth above.

abl.dsp.squaresync~.name: abl.dsp.squaresync~
abl.dsp.squaresync~.description: A synced dual square wave oscillator

abl.dsp.stereolfo~.name: abl.dsp.stereolfo~
abl.dsp.stereolfo~.description: An LFO with two output channels.

abl.dsp.subosc~.name: abl.dsp.subosc~
abl.dsp.subosc~.description: An oscillator with waveshaping and an auxiliary sub bass oscillator.

abl.dsp.swarm~.name: abl.dsp.swarm~
abl.dsp.swarm~.description: A swarm of oscillators with modulation and frequency spacing.

abl.dsp.tarp~.name: abl.dsp.tarp~
abl.dsp.tarp~.description: A kick/bass oscillator for creating 808 style sounds.

abl.dsp.tides~.name: abl.dsp.tides~
abl.dsp.tides~.description: Algorithmic Tides reverb from Live's Hybrid Reverb device.

abl.dsp.transform~.name: abl.dsp.transform~
abl.dsp.transform~.description: A collection of common signal modifiers.

abl.dsp.transientdesign~.name: abl.dsp.transientdesign~
abl.dsp.transientdesign~.description: Applies transient shaping on a signal given a reference signal.

abl.dsp.velvetnoise~.name: abl.dsp.velvetnoise~
abl.dsp.velvetnoise~.description:
A noise generator useful for noise convolution.
        
        Velvet noise is an impulse train where impulses are randomly positive and negative.
        The spacing between the impulses is derived from the number of samples that defines a temporal slice in which
        only one impulse exists. The impulse can be located anywhere within its slice. One characteristic of this
        impulse train is that even with a relatively low density, it has a fairly even spectrum.

abl.dsp.vowel~.name: abl.dsp.vowel~
abl.dsp.vowel~.description: A bank of five bandpass filters with frequencies and gains that correspond to the vowels AEIOU.

abl.dsp.wander~.name: abl.dsp.wander~
abl.dsp.wander~.description: Outputs a signal that ramps between random levels.

abl.dsp.waveshaper~.name: abl.dsp.waveshaper~
abl.dsp.waveshaper~.description: A saturation effect with control over the waveshaping transfer function.

jit.bang.name: jit.bang
jit.bang.description: Outputs a bang every time a frame is rendered by the attached context.

jit.framecount.name: jit.framecount
jit.framecount.description: Outputs the count of frames as they are rendered by the attached context.

jit.line.name: jit.line
jit.line.digest: Generate timed ramp synched with render context
jit.line.description: Generate ramps and line segements from one value to another within a specified amount of time, and output in synch with and timed by an external render context. jit.line functions as a drop in replacement for standard Max line object, but removes stuttering associated with unsynched output between the line timer and context frames.

jit.mo.field.name: jit.mo.field
jit.mo.field.digest: Field manipulator for 3 plane jit.mo streams
jit.mo.field.description: Field manipulator for 3 plane jit.mo streams. Deforms position output depending on distance from a defined spatial location. Can be used for sculpting effects and gravity-like animations.

jit.mo.fieldmask.name: jit.mo.fieldmask
jit.mo.fieldmask.digest: Field mask for 3 plane jit.mo streams
jit.mo.fieldmask.description: Field mask for 3 plane jit.mo streams. Calculates a mask value depending on distance from a defined spatial location. Can be used to perform arbitrary manipulations based on location

jit.mo.func.name: jit.mo.func
jit.mo.func.digest: Generate animated single dim matrices using a specified function
jit.mo.func.description: Generate animated single dim matrices using a specified function. Similar in nature to a sound oscillator, jit.mo.func can generate time-varying cell values across a matrix based on a given function.

arguments.name: Dimension
arguments.digest: Set the dimension (number of elements) of the output matrix

jit.mo.join.name: jit.mo.join
jit.mo.join.digest: Combine jit.mo streams and output a multi-plane matrix
jit.mo.join.description: Combine jit.mo streams and output a multi-plane matrix. Automatically connects to jit.world to drive animations. Inputs add multiple jit.mo/matrix inputs for additive control.

arguments.name: Inlet Count
arguments.digest: Set the number of inlets and planecount of output

arguments.name: Dimension
arguments.digest: Set the dimension (number of elements) of the output matrix and any attached

jit.mo.time.name: jit.mo.time
jit.mo.time.digest: Outputs float time values using specified mode for realtime animation
jit.mo.time.description: Outputs float time values using specified mode for realtime animation. Can be used to generate control functions in sync with other jit.world and jit.mo objects, time delta between frames, or accumulated running time.

live.adsrui.name: live.adsrui
live.adsrui.description: The live.adsrui object is a user interface for defining the shape of an envelope using the classic Attack, Decay, Sustain, Release model. Each segment has an individually definable length and exponential slope. This object is designed to work seamlessly with its companion object, live.adsr~.

live.adsr~.name: live.adsr~
live.adsr~.description: Use the live.adsr~ object to create sample-accurate Attack-Decay-Sustain-Release signal envelopes. Pair live.adsr~ with the live.adsrui object for graphical control over each of its stages.

live.scope~.name: live.scope~
live.scope~.description: Use live.scope~ to visualize an audio signal. This object can be used as an alternative to scope~.

mira.motion.name: mira.motion
mira.motion.digest: Receive data from iPad accelerometer, gyroscope and magnetometer.
mira.motion.description: Mira.motion outputs readings from the device accelerometer, magnetometer and gyroscope. On compatible devices, these data are available both as raw readings from onboard sensors and as postprocessed information that accounts for sensor bias and drift. Each reading is output from the mira.motion object in the form (data-type) (data-args) (device-name), where data-type is one of rawaccel, rawgyro, rawmagnet, rotationrate, gravity, accel, compass or orientation; data-args is between three and four numbers containing the value of the data; and device-name is the name of the mobile device as defined in Mira. The mira.mo abstractions can assist in filtering and formatting the output of mira.motion.

mira.multitouch.name: mira.multitouch
mira.multitouch.digest: Receive touch and gesture data from touchscreen.
mira.multitouch.description: Mira.multitouch receives touch and gesture data from a multitouch device. It will output the position and state of as many touches as are supported by the device. When enabled, it will also output a message when a device gesture is recognized, as well as the state of that gesture. Mira.multitouch can recognize tap, swipe, rotation and pinch gestures. The mira.mt abstrations are useful for processing and filtering the messages output by mira.multitouch.
